name: Ribir
description: Declarative, reactive, pure-Rust GUI framework with wgpu rendering and a non-intrusive state model

latest-stable-version.comment: 0.3.0 (2024-08-26) is the most recent non-prerelease. Active development is on 0.4.0-alpha.* (latest 0.4.0-alpha.65, 2026-04-21).

maintenance-status.comment: Weekly 0.4.0-alpha releases through April 2026; 69 releases total; 1,830+ commits on master.

maturity.comment: Core framework described as "basically stable" by maintainers; widget library self-described as in "rough stage" with unstable APIs. No 1.0 release yet (current track is 0.4.0-alpha).

corporate-backing.comment: No corporate sponsor identified in README, website, or crates.io metadata.

implementation.comment: Pure Rust; wgpu + winit + lyon (path tessellation) with no C/C++ GUI toolkit dependency.

rendering-mode.comment: Persistent widget tree with point-to-point compile-time-generated update paths on state mutation.

reactivity-model.comment: "Non-intrusive state" converts data to listenable states that trigger fine-grained UI updates on mutation — a signals-style reactivity model.

thread-model.comment: winit + wgpu based; UI runs on the main thread per standard winit event-loop constraints. Not explicitly documented beyond the underlying stack.

renderer.comment: Default (and primary) backend is wgpu; a webgl feature exists for WASM.

hidpi-support.comment: Inherits HiDPI handling from winit + wgpu; framework is resolution-independent (path-based).

vector-rendering.comment: Painter converts the view to 2D paths and tessellates them via lyon.

custom-shaders.comment: No documented API for user-provided GPU shaders or custom render passes in README or docs; left null pending verification.

embedded-custom-rendering.comment: No documented API for embedding a wgpu/OpenGL viewport inside the UI; left null pending verification.

linux-x11.comment: Supported via winit; desktop Linux is in CI.

linux-wayland.comment: Supported via winit's Wayland backend; no Wayland-specific caveats called out.

ios.comment: README marks iOS as 🚧 (compiles but not fully tested/adapted). Per RESEARCH.md guidance, only mark true when officially supported and tested.

android.comment: README marks Android as 🚧 (compiles but not fully tested/adapted).

web-wasm.comment: Web is in CI; live wordle_game demo is hosted at ribir.org/wordle_game/.

embedded-no-std.comment: Depends on winit, wgpu, and tokio — std-only; no no_std target advertised.

authoring-style.comment: Primary authoring is via Rust macros (fn_widget!, pipe!, rdl!). A macro-free imperative/builder path is also supported for users who opt out of the DSL.

hot-reload.comment: No hot-reload feature documented in README or docs; macro-based authoring would require recompile.

layout-system.comment: Layout is "inspired by Flutter sublinear layout but not the same" — widget-driven sublinear constraint propagation rather than CSS flexbox/grid.

styling-approach.comment: Theme struct with support for different themes per sub-tree and runtime modification; styles are also expressible inline on widgets.

theme-switching.comment: README explicitly lists runtime theme modification.

accessibility.comment: Independent 2025 survey found Windows Narrator could not read any text in a Ribir window. No AccessKit integration documented.

internationalization.comment: No built-in i18n/localization layer documented. Basic IME input exists but is described as rough by maintainers, and the 2025 survey reported significant IME defects.

widget-breadth.comment: Maintainers describe the widget library as ~20+ basic widgets in a "rough stage" with unstable APIs; no tables, trees, rich text, markdown, or charts documented.

rich-text-markdown.comment: Only basic text typography and IME input; no rich-text or markdown widget documented.

native-menus.comment: No native menu-bar or context-menu integration documented.

file-dialogs.comment: No built-in file dialogs documented; users would need to pull in rfd or similar themselves.

custom-widgets.comment: Framework exposes four explicit composition patterns (function widget, Compose, Render, ComposeChild) with docs; implementing a custom Render widget requires understanding the painter/layout traits but is a documented path.

async-runtime-compatible.comment: Tokio is a direct dependency of the top-level crate; futures/rxrust are also used internally.

system-tray.comment: No system-tray integration documented in README or website; left null pending verification.

notifications.comment: No native-notification integration documented in README or website; left null pending verification.

clipboard.comment: arboard (cross-platform clipboard crate) is a direct dependency of ribir, indicating built-in clipboard support.

drag-and-drop.comment: No OS-level drag-and-drop integration documented in README or website; left null pending verification.

embedded-webview.comment: Ribir is a pure-Rust widget framework; no webview embedding capability documented.

multi-window.comment: No explicit multi-window support documented in README or website; left null pending verification.

binary-size.comment: No independently measured hello-world binary size available; would require a measurement pass.

startup-time.comment: No independently measured startup time available.

memory-footprint.comment: No independently measured idle RSS available.

idle-redraw.comment: Point-to-point view-update strategy with compile-time generated update paths — UI redraws only when listenable state mutates.

dependency-count.comment: lib.rs reports ~78-125MB installed and ~2M SLoC in the dependency tree via wgpu, winit, tokio, and rxrust — implies heavy (>200) transitive crates.

documentation-quality.comment: Tutorial-based docs with versioned paths (0.1.x, 0.2.x, Next) and bilingual English/Chinese content; API docs on docs.rs. Adequate but not at the depth of Slint/Tauri/Dioxus books.

api-ergonomics.comment: Idiomatic Rust macros (fn_widget!, pipe!, rdl!) and a composition system, but with unstable widget APIs and some learning curve around the four widget composition patterns.

examples-count.comment: 8 example projects in the official examples/ folder: attachments, counter, gallery, messages, pomodoro, storybook, todos, wordle_game.

interactive-playground.comment: Official WASM demo (Wordle-style game) hosted on the project site; no general-purpose REPL/playground.

ide-tooling.comment: No Ribir-specific LSP, formatter, or inspector documented; authoring is in-Rust so rust-analyzer handles syntax but there is no framework-aware tooling.

github-stars.comment: Approx 1.7k stars as of 2026-04-24; star counts drift.

crates-io-downloads.comment: Exact crates.io download count not captured during this research pass.

last-release.comment: Most recent release is v0.4.0-alpha.65 on 2026-04-21. The most recent non-prerelease was 0.3.0 on 2024-08-26.

notable-production-users.comment: Self-listed on the Ribir website; no other widely-known shipping apps identified.

community-size.comment: Few active contributors outside the core RibirX team; 37 forks and 25 watchers as of research date.

ecosystem-crates.comment: Ecosystem is essentially the RibirX-maintained sub-crates (ribir_core, ribir_gpu, ribir_text, ribir_widgets, ribir_dev_helper); no third-party theme, widget, or integration crate ecosystem identified.
